query_normalized: developing new methods to synthesize high-performance membranes using sustainable materials chemistry
rationale: This paper satisfies criteria (a) and (b) as it provides empirical evidence on the performance (water permeability and salt rejection) of a specific membrane material (graphyne), which is a direct measurement of the "high-performance" variable the user aims to synthesize, even though the material is carbon-based rather than explicitly labeled "sustainable" in the abstract. It serves as a critical baseline and mechanism study for the domain of next-generation desalination membranes.
